compiled_state.title: As a host-project maintainer, I can rely on CI fetching the right DSO plugin version via the 3-tier resolution chain with fail-closed semantics
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement plugins/dso/scripts/resolve-dso-version.sh that walks a 3-tier version resolution chain, plus the CI workflow Resolve-and-fetch DSO plugin assets step that invokes it and clones the resolved version with sentinel-based integrity verification and fail-closed semantics.

**Why**: DSO plugin agent files and helper scripts live in the plugin repo, not host project repos. Host-project CI must fetch them at runtime. The 3-tier chain prioritizes the version the developer is using locally (parity), with explicit fallbacks.

**Scope**:
- IN: resolve-dso-version.sh helper walking 3 tiers (project Claude config plugin-tracking file → ci.dso_plugin_version in .claude/dso-config.conf → marketplace.json HEAD stable dso channel); CI workflow Resolve-and-fetch step; clone target ${RUNNER_TEMP:-$(mktemp -d)}/dso/<version>/; sentinel file recording resolved version + commit SHA; existence-check uses sentinel; fail-closed when all 3 tiers fail with diagnostic naming which tier(s) failed; marketplace.json fallback rejects non-stable channels.
- OUT: auto-detect runner mode (Story 5); host-project E2E (Story 7).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, resolve-dso-version.sh walks the 3-tier chain and emits the resolved version + source tier to stdout/INFO log.
  ← Satisfies: "tier 1 reads the project's Claude per-project plugin-tracking file ... tier 2 reads ci.dso_plugin_version ... tier 3 fetches marketplace.json from navapbc/digital-service-orchestra@HEAD and reads the stable dso channel only"
- When this story is complete, the CI workflow Resolve-and-fetch step clones the resolved version into ${RUNNER_TEMP:-$(mktemp -d)}/dso/<version>/ and writes a sentinel file containing the resolved version + commit SHA after the clone completes.
  ← Satisfies: "Clone targets ${RUNNER_TEMP:-$(mktemp -d)}/dso/<version>/ ... Clone success is gated by a sentinel file written only after fetch completes"
- When this story is complete, on subsequent runs the existence-check uses the sentinel (not the directory) AND verifies the recorded commit SHA matches the resolved version's actual SHA before reusing the cached clone.
  ← Satisfies: sentinel-based integrity (cache reuse must validate not just exist)
- When this story is complete, all-three-tiers-failure exits non-zero with a diagnostic naming which tier(s) failed and why.
  ← Satisfies: "Fail-closed: all-three-tiers-fail exits non-zero with diagnostic indicating which tier(s) failed"
- When this story is complete, the marketplace.json fallback (tier 3) rejects with explicit error if the stable dso channel entry is absent or malformed — does NOT fall back to dso-dev.
  ← Satisfies: "stable dso channel only ... resolver MUST NOT fall back to dso-dev channel"
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Security] Fetching remote git assets requires version pinning to immutable tags; the sentinel records the commit SHA so subsequent runs verify against the resolved tag's actual SHA. If the upstream tag is force-moved, the SHA mismatch causes a re-clone.
- [Implicit-shared-state] Sentinel file should record version + commit SHA, not just exist. A bare existence check on the directory or sentinel is insufficient — concurrent CI jobs may have written a partial clone or the cache may have been partially evicted.
- [Implicit-shared-state] Concurrent CI jobs racing on shared $RUNNER_TEMP/dso/<version>/: implement atomic clone-and-rename (clone to a per-job tmpdir, then rename into place) OR use a per-job isolated path (e.g., $RUNNER_TEMP/dso/<version>-<github-job-id>/). Without this, parallel matrix jobs will corrupt each other's clones.
- [Conflicting-assumptions] marketplace.json HEAD pin (Tier 3) is not immutable — it advances on each scripts/release.sh run. A re-run weeks later may resolve to a different version. Document this non-determinism in the resolver's INFO log AND surface the resolved version in the CI step output so the resolved version is recorded in the PR check artifact for reproducibility.
- [Consumer-impact] PR-author-controllable version resolution (Tier 1 reads the project's Claude config plugin-tracking file, which is in-repo) is a CI-runner code-execution vector if a malicious PR points to a forked DSO repo. Trust boundary: resolver MUST validate that the resolved repo URL matches navapbc/digital-service-orchestra (the canonical upstream) — reject any non-upstream ref. Restrict resolution to the official DSO repo + signed/known refs.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
